title: rosa tomentosa subvar. dimorpha
intro: Rosa tomentosa subvar. dimorpha, also known as the hairy rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Rosa tomentosa subvar. dimorpha include treating digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns.
